flow.auth.password_login.description: The user signs in with a login (email/username) and password. The endpoint is enabled by the AUTH_PASSWORD_LOGIN setting. Failed attempts lead to progressive lockout (423 with retry_after). If the user has TOTP enabled and the PASSWORD_LOGIN_STEP_UP setting is active (default: yes), TOTP_REQUIRED with a challenge_token is returned instead of tokens — the session is issued only after the authenticator code is verified.
flow.auth.password_login.step.0.note: The user enters their login and password on the login form
flow.auth.password_login.step.1.note: Verify the password; 423 when locked out; with TOTP enabled and PASSWORD_LOGIN_STEP_UP — a TOTP_REQUIRED response with a challenge_token
flow.auth.password_login.step.2.note: Optional step (only on TOTP_REQUIRED): exchange the challenge_token and the authenticator code for a JWT session
flow.auth.password_login.title: Password login (+ optional TOTP)
flow.auth.passwordless_login.description: An anonymous user receives a one-time code by email and exchanges it for a JWT session (cookies + a token pair in the response body). Requesting the code again is rate-limited (30 seconds between sends; 429/422 when exceeded); after a series of wrong codes the address is temporarily locked. If the address was not registered, the first successful login creates a new user (status=REGISTERED instead of LOGGED_IN).
flow.auth.passwordless_login.step.0.note: The user enters their email on the login form
flow.auth.passwordless_login.step.1.note: Request a one-time code by email; 429 on rate limit, 422 when the address is locked
flow.auth.passwordless_login.step.2.note: Exchange the code for a JWT session; a wrong code decrements the attempt counter
flow.auth.passwordless_login.step.3.note: Emitted on first login — the profile and workspace are created by subscribers
flow.auth.passwordless_login.title: Passwordless login (email OTP)
flow.auth.step_up_verification.descripti: THE reference flow of the step-up verification contract (stapel_core.verification, see flows-and-verification.md §2) — clients of any service implement it once and reuse it for every endpoint protected by @requires_verification. The cycle: the protected endpoint responds 403 with a structured verification envelope (challenge_id, scope, factors, expires_at) → the client reads the challenge, picks an available factor (factors are interchangeable: otp_email, otp_phone, totp, passkey all close one challenge), initiates it and completes the check → repeats the original request. The grant is stored server-side (cache, user+scope key, TTL=max_age); stateless clients may instead send the X-Verification-Token header from the completion response. After MAX_ATTEMPTS wrong attempts the challenge burns out (423) — call the original endpoint again for a new challenge.
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.0.no: The client calls the protected endpoint and receives 403 with a verification envelope: challenge_id, scope, factors, expires_at
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.1.no: Read the challenge: the scope and the factors filtered down to those actually available to the user; 404 for a foreign/expired challenge
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.2.no: Initiate the chosen factor: send a code (otp_email/otp_phone) or get WebAuthn options (passkey); totp needs no initiation
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.3.no: Complete the challenge with the factor proof; success = {verified, verification_token} + a server-side grant; 400 on a wrong code, 423 when the challenge burned out from brute force
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.4.no: Repeat the original request — the grant is already on the server; a stateless client sends the X-Verification-Token from the completion response
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.5.no: Optional: view your step-up preferences — one {scope, enabled} row per scope the user has touched (enabled=false disables a default_on scope, enabled=true enables an opt_in scope; strict endpoints ignore the preferences)
flow.auth.step_up_verification.step.6.no: Optional: change a {scope, enabled} preference. INVARIANT: disabling (enabled=false) is itself protected by @requires_verification(scope=verification.settings, level=default_on) — without a fresh grant a 403 with a verification envelope is returned; enabling requires no step-up confirmation. Both writes reset the policy cache in core
flow.auth.step_up_verification.title: Step-up verification on a protected endpoint (reference flow)
